title: The Eternal 'Predator' or Manda on Tanda at SRI: On Saving the 'USL-ism' of National Security
summary: The article criticizes proposals for the leadership of Romania's intelligence service (SRI), with UDMR's Kelemen Hunor suggesting Cătălin Predoiu and PSD countering with Claudiu Manda or Mihai Tudose. The author argues all candidates are unsuitable and that these proposals violate President Nicușor Dan's constitutional prerogative to nominate the SRI director, revealing the PSD-PNL-UDMR coalition's intent to maintain control over national security institutions.
talking_points: Kelemen Hunor's track record of proposing failed candidates, from Crin Antonescu for president to now Predoiu for SRI director
Predoiu is criticized for destroying the justice system through reforms that freed white-collar criminals and bringing public trust in the Interior Ministry to a minimum
PSD's counter-proposals of Claudiu Manda and Mihai Tudose are described as Dragnea loyalists unfit for intelligence leadership
The nominations violate President Nicușor Dan's exclusive constitutional prerogative to nominate the SRI director, despite parliamentary confirmation being required
The PSD-PNL-UDMR coalition uses a manipulation tactic: offering two bad options to force acceptance of the lesser evil
author: Radu Ştefăniță
